scheme_name: Distribution of Seeds of more productions verities / Hybrids verities Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area: Distribution of Minor and Micro Elements - Gujarat
page_title: Distribution of Seeds of more productions verities / Hybrids verities Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area: Distribution of Minor and Micro Elements - Gujarat

details: The scheme "Distribution of Seeds of more productive varieties / Hybrid varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area (TASP)" with a component "Distribution of Minor and Micro Elements" is introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims the farmers belonging to scheduled tribes and tribal cultivators residing outside tribal areas can achieve higher production of agricultural crops and improve their livelihood by distribution of minor and micro elements.
benefits: Distribution of minor and micro elements: The applicant receives a subsidy of 75% of the purchase price or ₹750/- per hectare, whichever is less, for minor and micro elements of a Government-approved grade under FCO 1985.
The assistance is limited to a maximum of two hectares per account.
Remark: As per conditions No. 5, 16, 17, 19 and 22 of
eligibility: The applicant should belong to a Scheduled Tribe.
The applicant should be a farmer.
The applicant should be located in tribal areas OR be a tribal cultivator residing outside tribal areas.
The scheme excludes farmers from Dang district.

offline: Approach the Gram Sevak in your village for access to the Distribution of Minor and Micro Elements under the scheme.

faqs.question: What is the benefit provided under the “Distribution of minor and micro elements” component?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides 75% of the purchase price or ₹750 per hectare, whichever is less, for purchasing minor and micro elements of government-approved grade.

faqs.question: What is the maximum land area eligible for subsidy under this component?
faqs.answer: The subsidy is available for a maximum of 2 hectares per account.

faqs.question: Which type of minor and micro elements are eligible under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Only government-approved grade minor and micro elements under the Fertilizer Control Order (FCO) 1985 are eligible.

faqs.question: How often can I receive benefits for minor and micro elements?
faqs.answer: You can receive this benefit every year, subject to the scheme’s conditions.

faqs.question: Who can apply for the “Distribution of minor and micro elements” subsidy?
faqs.answer: Scheduled Tribe farmers, except those from Dang district, can apply for this benefit.

faqs.question: Can farmers living outside tribal areas apply for this component?
faqs.answer: Yes, tribal cultivators residing outside tribal areas are also eligible.

faqs.question: What is the frequency of disbursement for this subsidy?
What documents do I need to apply for this component?
faqs.answer: You need to submit your 7/12 land record, Aadhaar card, and bank passbook or cancelled cheque.

faqs.question: How will I receive the subsidy amount?
faqs.answer: The assistance is provided through the scheme’s subsidy process managed by the department via your Gram Sevak.

faqs.question: Can I get the subsidy if I have more than 2 hectares of land?
faqs.answer: You can receive subsidy only for up to 2 hectares per account, even if you own more land.
